bib.title: Fairness in Logic Languages based on Shared Dataspace.
bib.citation: ICLP Workshop on Blackboard-Based Logic Programming, 1993
bib.author: Mauro Gaspari and Laura Semini
bib.conference: ICLP Workshop on Blackboard-Based Logic Programming
bib.abstract: We present a study on the fairness properties of a family of concurrent logic languages based on shared dataspace. These languages use a blackboard as a repository of logic tuples shared among di erent agents, communication and synchronization are performed using a set of rules which express read and delete conditions on the blackboard. We de ne a kernel language to represent a signi cant subset of the languages of the family, and we model it through a transition system. We distinguish two sources of non determinism, and correspondently we propose the schedulers ensuring the relative fairness properties. We prove that the composition of these schedulers guarantees a global weak fairness, while a global strong-fairness is ensured exploiting program transformation techniques.
